sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाचधर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः । मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
hindiTranslation: धृतराष्ट्र बोले   हे संजय  धर्मभूमि कुरुक्षेत्र में युद्ध की इच्छा से इकट्ठे हुए मेरेे और पाण्डु के पुत्रों ने भी क्या किया ॥
englishTranslation: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do, assembled on the holy field of Kurukshetra and eager for battle?
essence: The war begins in a question, not a sword strike.
speaker: Dhritarashtra
simpleMeaning: Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya what happened when both sides gathered on the battlefield, ready to fight.
simpleInsight: Before any action begins, uncertainty can feel heavier than the battle itself.
detailedMeaning: Dhritarashtra, blind and anxious, asks Sanjaya about the moment before war begins on Kurukshetra. His own sons and the Pandavas have assembled, but the outcome is still hidden. This opening question sets the whole Gita in motion.
modernRelevance: You sit outside a hospital room, waiting for news after a family conflict has finally exploded. Nothing has been said yet, but everything has already changed.
translationLiteral: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच (Dhritarashtra said) / धर्मक्षेत्रे (on the field of duty) / कुरुक्षेत्रे (at Kurukshetra) / समवेता (assembled) / युयुत्सवः (desiring to fight) / मामकाः (my people) / पाण्डवाः (the sons of Pandu) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / किम् (what) / अकुर्वत (did they do) / सञ्जय (O Sanjaya)
insight: It frames the entire Gita as a witness account of a crisis already in motion.
